nouns.summary: The text states that “our sicknesses” were carried/bore by him, meaning he took responsibility for the people’s illnesses.
It says that “our pains” (the suffering of the people) were borne/absorbed by him, indicating he endured what the people were experiencing.
The phrase connects “their suffering/burdens” with what he bore, presenting his participation as taking on the weight of their affliction.
The people “thought” him to be “stricken/afflicted,” treating his condition as one of being afflicted.
The text says the people considered him “struck,” implying they believed he had been hit/struck down as his perceived condition.
It specifies that the people thought he was “struck by God,” attributing his perceived state of being struck to divine action.
The text states that the people believed him to be “afflicted/oppressed,” describing another perceived condition they assigned to him.
